$comment: Cross-implementation test vectors for the Passkey Access Vault ERC. An implementation that reproduces these bytes interoperates. These are NOT examples — they are the conformance bar. CC0.

walletKey.$comment: K = HKDF-SHA256(IKM=prf, salt=hkdfSalt, info=walletInfo, L=32). K IS the secp256k1 private key.

slotId.$comment: keccak256(address_bytes20 || decoded_credentialId). The address binding is a privacy requirement.

slotWrappingKey.$comment: W = HKDF-SHA256(IKM=prf_of_THIS_door, salt=hkdfSalt, info=`${slotInfoPrefix}|${lowercase_address}|${slotId}`, L=32). Both info inputs are PUBLIC — that is what lets an enroller derive W alone and never receive K.

metaKey.$comment: MetaKey = HKDF-SHA256(IKM=K, salt=hkdfSalt, info=slotMetaInfo, L=32). Derived from K, not a PRF.

blob.$comment: version(1) || iv(12) || AES-GCM(W, K)(48) = 61 bytes. The iv here is FIXED so the vector is deterministic; a real write MUST use a random iv.

userHandle.$comment: founding = [0x01][32 random] (33B). access = [0x02][20B address][8B BE chainId] (29B).

sas.$comment: 6 digits from SHA-256(bPub || aPub || nonce). bPub is the INITIATOR (the enroller).
